n1.speaker: narrator
n1.line: Evening. The whole district, assembled. Bunting overhead, lanterns lit early for the occasion. Warren Holt gives the speech he has rehearsed for eight years, and means every word of it.

n2.speaker: warren
n2.line: Fifty years ago, our founders sealed away the things they loved best, and trusted us to be the kind of district worth opening them for. Julian — the key, and the honor, are yours.

n3.speaker: narrator
n3.line: Julian Vale takes the Ceremonial Key. The lock turns — smooth, well-oiled, Ida's lifetime of care in one quiet click. The vault door swings open.

n4.speaker: narrator
n4.line: The crowd leans in. Silence. The empty vault holds the stage for a long, long three seconds.

n5.speaker: poppy
n5.line: That's... that's not one of my contingency cards.

n7.speaker: julian
n7.line: Perhaps... perhaps there's been some mistake with the— the masonry.

n8.speaker: narrator
n8.line: The ring on Julian's finger spins; he stares into the vault a beat too long.

n11.speaker: narrator
n11.line: Gary's long fingers find the brass badge inside his lapel, the way they always do when the floor tilts.

n12.speaker: gary
n12.line: Okay, Grandpa. Remember who you are.
